response:
source
The function begins by initializing two accumulator variables to track the sum of squares and the product of cubes, setting the sum to zero and the product to one as required for an empty input list.

***
It then iterates through each integer within the provided slice, calculating the square of the current number by multiplying it by itself and adding this result to the running sum of squares.

***
Simultaneously, the function computes the cube of the current number by multiplying the integer by itself twice, and multiplies this value into the running product of cubes to maintain the cumulative total.

***
If the input slice contains no elements, the loop will not execute, naturally leaving the sum of squares at zero and the product of cubes at one, which satisfies the edge case condition specified in the requirements.

***
Once the loop completes processing every item in the set, the function packages the final calculated sum of squares and the final product of cubes into a fixed-size array of two integers.

***
The completed function returns this array, ensuring that the first element represents the total summation of squares and the second element represents the total multiplication of cubes for the entire dataset.
